observations: REV3 REDESIGN (human-authorized attempt 4; breaker 027-contract extended attempts=3/limit=5 per run_state.circuit_breaker) IS FAITHFUL. Sub-check (1)'s physical-line-join lineage (rev0 whole-paragraph, rev1 capped, rev2 cap-free-line-boundary) is RETIRED and replaced by a format-agnostic NORMALIZE -> SEGMENT -> CHECK pipeline: STAGE 1 (one python3 pass) strips HTML/XML tag spans `<[^>]*>` -> single SPACE then collapses all whitespace/newlines to single spaces; STAGE 2 segments via `re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', text)` (boundary only after ./!/? + whitespace/EOS; never on ;/:; never inside `GIP.md`-style filename tokens because the `.` there is followed by a letter, not whitespace); STAGE 3 writes each segment as its own line and calls the UNMODIFIED vlib_no_stale_payload EXACTLY ONCE. This is a strictly MORE faithful realization of spec F-027 sub-check (1) ('no stale 3-dir enumeration survives ANYWHERE') than any physical-line heuristic, because correctness no longer depends on where authors insert line breaks.

REV2 EVALUATOR BLOCKER (.agent/qa/027-contract-review.json, revision_reviewed=2, issues[0], severity high: HTML `.

` sentences never satisfy a physical-line close condition, so whole un-blank-line-separated `

` blocks collapse into one join-group -> a genuine false-PASS on docs/guide/index.html's own dominant `.

`-per-sentence convention) IS CLOSED AT THE ROOT. Under REV3, Stage 1 replaces `

` with a space, turning `...only.

Elsewhere...tools/...` into `...only. Elsewhere...tools/...`, exposing the ./whitespace boundary Stage 2 requires -- the sentences segment correctly regardless of original physical-line layout. This exceeds the evaluator's minimum required_fix (which asked only to teach the join to recognize a trailing block-level closing tag OR an equivalent HTML-aware rule): the redesign removes the physical-line dependency entirely rather than patching one more boundary case.

REV2 EVALUATOR missing_acceptance_coverage (a dedicated fixture styled on docs/guide/index.html's own `.

` convention proving an HTML-styled stale enumeration is still caught when adjacent, same block, to an unrelated tools/ `

`) IS CLOSED: new fixture scope.files_create[4] tools/fixtures/stale_payload_html.html reproduces exactly the `...only.

Elsewhere...tools/...

` same-source-line shape, paired with a FAIL assertion (verification[17]) requiring `[C10] FAIL` + `payload-set` tag + the reconstructed stale sentence AND explicitly asserting the unrelated tools/ sentence text is NOT in the offending output, plus a neutral-mktemp anti-cheat entry (verification[18]). Reachable via the (already-contracted) new `*.html` --target case. Additive soundness, not scope creep.

SCOPE UNCHANGED and INTACT: scope.files_modify is exactly the 4 files (tools/validate.sh, docs/guide/index.html, methodology/06_release_train.md, standard/GIP.md); scope.files_create is now 7 fixtures (stale_payload.md, stale_payload_cosentence.md, stale_payload_longwrap.md, stale_payload_semicolon.md, stale_payload_html.html, bad_discovery_order.md, version_drift.html), ALL under tools/fixtures/. The AT9 scope guard (verification[8]: vlib_scope_guard over the 4 modify paths + the 'tools/fixtures/' prefix) covers the new .html fixture with NO allow-list edit. Nothing else added.

COMPOSITIONAL DISCIPLINE HOLDS: non_goals[2] keeps tools/verify_lib.sh untouched; the normalize/segment pipeline is PREPROCESSING inside check_c10_consistency composing the vetted, unmodified vlib_no_stale_payload exactly once per file (F-023 primitive unchanged across all four attempts). Using python3 for the normalize/segment stage introduces NO new runtime dependency: the contract correctly states python3 is already a hard, fail-closed dependency of tools/validate.sh (also used by sub-check (3)'s NIZAM.json read and by C4's jsonschema stack). PIPEFAIL SAFETY is a structural benefit -- the single python3 invocation exits 0 on ordinary/empty input, so sub-check (1) carries none of the grep|head|cut pipefail hazard that sub-checks (2)/(3) still (unchanged) guard against.

ACCEPTANCE MAPPING INTACT: all 10 feature_list_004 F-027 acceptance tests map 1:1 to verification[0..9]. The 17 ADDITIONAL entries (verification[10..26]) are additive soundness: neutral-filename anti-cheats, the rev0/rev1/rev2 regression fixtures (cosentence, longwrap, semicolon, HTML), the two 3-mode pinning proofs (payload 9/0; real-guide *.html target PASS), and the NDEBT-003(a/b) + GIP remediation proofs. final_verdict.summary:
APPROVED (rev3, the human-authorized REDESIGN of sub-check 1). The physical-line-join lineage (rev0/1/2) is retired entirely and replaced by a format-agnostic NORMALIZE (python3: strip HTML tag spans -> space, collapse all whitespace to single spaces) -> SEGMENT (re.split on `(?<=[.!?])\s+`; never ;/:; never inside filename tokens) -> CHECK (the UNMODIFIED vlib_no_stale_payload once per segment file) pipeline that never inspects physical line boundaries. This faithfully and root-cause closes the rev2 evaluator blocker (the HTML `.

`-collapsed false-pass on docs/guide/index.html's own dominant sentence convention): Stage 1 turns `only.

Elsewhere` into `only. Elsewhere`, exposing the ./whitespace boundary Stage 2 splits on -- exceeding the evaluator's minimum required_fix, which asked only for an HTML-tag-aware close extension. The rev2 missing_acceptance_coverage is closed by the new tools/fixtures/stale_payload_html.html fixture (reproducing the exact same-source-line `

` shape) with a FAIL assertion (verification[17]) that also asserts the unrelated tools/ sentence is NOT merged into the offending output, plus a neutral-mktemp anti-cheat (verification[18]). Every prior-approved property holds unchanged: scope is still 4 modify + fixtures-only creates (now 7, ALL under the already-allow-listed tools/fixtures/); verify_lib.sh untouched (non_goals[2]); python3 is a pre-existing hard dependency, so no new dependency is introduced; all 10 F-027 acceptance tests map 1:1 to verification[0..9]; C8 per-file bumps, the three narrow content fixes, the NDEBT-003 fix-now + no-fourth-subcheck (NDEBT-005) decisions, P3 discovery-order scoping, the External Anchor Rule, the forced new `*.html` --target case, and 3-mode pinning (default 10/0, payload 9/0) are all unchanged and correct. Verification 25->27, evidence_convention 027-verify-27.txt, estimated_lines 200->215 are reasonable additive deltas. Zero blocking discrepancies. Non-blocking: (1) pre_existing_finding_gip's corrected close-mechanism prose is right on the load-bearing point (segment closes at the '...local mutation.' period + whitespace; verified against the real GIP.md L103-106, contains standard/+templates/+schema/ without tools/, fix genuinely forced) but still mis-identifies the immediately-following sentence as item 4 'Detecting staleness.' when item 3's own second sentence intervenes -- post-hoc narration only, the segmenter is fully specified generically and verification[25] asserts on the real span content + a live [C10] PASS, failing closed; (2) carried recommendation to amend feature_list F-027 AT9 to the corrected 5-path scope in-place; (3) spec-P2's stale version literals are non-binding under the External Anchor Rule.
